title: Claude vs GPT-4 vs Gemini: honest comparison after 6 months
post:
I've used Claude, GPT-4, and Gemini extensively for 6 months. Here's the unfiltered truth:

Claude:
Best for: nuanced reasoning, writing, analysis, and long documents. Its constitutional AI approach makes it genuinely helpful without being sycophantic. Loses steam on very recent information.

GPT-4:
Best for: coding, problem-solving, and general tasks. The ecosystem advantage is real—more tools, more integrations. Sometimes too eager to please.

Gemini:
Best for: multimodal tasks and Google ecosystem integration. Still maturing. Context window is genuinely impressive but not always leveraged well.

My daily stack: Claude for writing and analysis, GPT-4 for coding and problem-solving, Gemini for research and multimodal tasks.

No single winner. The right tool depends on the task.

Which model is your daily driver?
